sections.paragraph:
f.-

(1) bone; so called from its firmness and strength; see the root No. 2. Arab. عَظْمُ, Gen 2:23 Exo 12:46 Num 9:12, etc. Plur. עֲצָמִים constr. עַצְמֵי Psa 6:3 31:11 32:3 more often also עֲצָמֹות Psa 51:10 Pro 14:30 often used of the bones of the dead (compare יָדֹות, בַּפֹּות), Exo 13:19 Jos 24:32 -2 Samuel 21:1214 ; 2Ki 23:14, 18 2Ki 23:18, 20 2Ki 23:20.

(2) body, bodily form, Lam 4:7.

(3) Followed by a genit.; it is used instead of the pronoun itself (compare syn. גֶּרֶם No. 3, and Arab. عين eye, himself); but only used of things, e.g. בְּעֶצֶם הַיֹּום הַזֶּה in that very day, Gen 7:13 17:23 , 26 Gen 17:26. Exo 24:10, כְּעֶצֶם הַשָּׁמַיִם “as the heaven itself.” Job 21:23, בְּעֵצֶם תֻּמֹּו “in his uprightness itself.”

(4) [Ezem, Azem], pr.n. of a town in the tribe of Simeon, Jos 15:29 19:3 1Ch 4:29.
